text.title: GraphRAG Retrieval Strategies
text.summary: Three retrieval strategies (local, global, hybrid), query classification, and context assembly rules for GraphRAG query execution.

section.title: Query Classification

rule.statement: Classify every query as LOCAL, GLOBAL, or HYBRID before retrieval; route to the corresponding strategy.
rule.rationale: Local and global searches use fundamentally different backends. Misrouting a global query to local search returns an empty or irrelevant subgraph; misrouting a local query to global wastes 2000+ tokens per community on summaries that don't contain the answer.

section.reference: Query classification prompt returning LOCAL / GLOBAL / HYBRID

section.title: Local Search

rule.statement: Cap graph traversal depth at 2 hops for agent tool calls; 3-hop queries return too much context for reliable synthesis.
rule.rationale: At 3 hops, subgraph size grows exponentially. The LLM context fills with tangentially related entities, reducing synthesis precision.

example.note: Minimal local search using Neo4j APOC path expansion
example.code:
from neo4j import GraphDatabase
def local_search(driver, entity_name: str, hops: int = 2) -> list[dict]: with driver.session() as session: result = session.run( """ MATCH (e:Entity {name: $name}) CALL apoc.path.subgraphNodes(e, {maxLevel: $hops}) YIELD node MATCH (node)-[r]-(neighbor) RETURN node.name, type(r), neighbor.name, r.description LIMIT 50 """, name=entity_name, hops=hops, ) return [dict(record) for record in result]

section.reference: Traversal patterns including 1-hop, 2-hop, weighted, path-finding, and hybrid vector+graph

rule.statement: Always cache global search results; regenerate only when corpus changes. Global search is too expensive to run per-query in real time.
rule.rationale: Map-reduce over 1000 community summaries at 2000 tokens each = 2M tokens per query. Without caching, a single global search in a ReAct loop can exhaust a session's token budget.

antipattern.description: Running global search inside a ReAct agent loop without result caching.
antipattern.reason: Each iteration burns 2000+ tokens per community summary. Three loop iterations on a corpus with 500 communities costs 3M tokens.

rule.statement: For hybrid search, start vector search, then expand matched nodes by 1-2 hops in the graph to capture related entities missed by semantic similarity.
rule.rationale: Vector search finds semantically similar text; graph traversal finds structurally connected entities. Combined, they surface entities mentioned in context but not in the query vocabulary.

rule.statement: Normalize entity names before every graph lookup; graph databases return empty results without error when names don't match exactly.
rule.rationale: An agent interpreting empty results as "no relevant information" will hallucinate. Silent empty returns are the #1 cause of GraphRAG agent hallucination in practice.
